LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WAYNE VALAIRS' MOTION TO STAY CIVIL ACTION UNTIL DISPOSITION OF CRIMINAL PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED IN PART. SEE MINUTES AND/OR TRANSCRIPT FOR DETAILS. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WAYNE VALAIRS' Motion to stay civil action until disposition of criminal proceedings is granted in part. Absent agreement of the parties or further order of the court, plaintiffs may not propound any discovery seeking information from Mr. Valaris. While Mr. Valaris' interest in protecting his 5th Amendment right against self-incrimination requires that no discovery be taken from him until the conclusion of the criminal case, at this point there is no need for a broader stay. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-13-2016 Continued To Jul-13-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
